news.headline: XRP price down 9%, but traders expect rally. XRP at $2.26, $129.08B market cap. Bitcoin at $98,630.61, $1.95T market cap. Ethereum at $3,474.60, $418.8B market cap. Bull cycle increased XRP 600x, potential $168 peak. More on Benzinga.com.
news.summary: Despite a 9% decrease in price, traders of XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) are maintaining a positive outlook, with many choosing to hold onto their positions in anticipation of an upward trend. XRP is currently priced at $2.26 with a market cap of $129.08 billion, showing an 8.8% decline in the last 24 hours and a 6.3% decrease over the past 7 days. Comparatively, Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is valued at $98,630.61 with a market cap of $1.95 trillion, experiencing a 5.8% drop in the last day and a 2.8% decrease over the week. Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) is priced at $3,474.60 with a market cap of $418.8 billion, reflecting a 10.5% decline in the last 24 hours and an 11.8% decrease over the past 7 days. Notable crypto trader Javon Marks pointed out XRP's previous surge of 600x and speculates the potential for a similar performance, suggesting a climb of 6,800% to approximately $168.

news.headline: Federal Reserve meeting affects Treasury yields, S&P 500; Trump's agenda a factor, stocks may be 'sloppy.'Characters: 101
news.summary: The recent Federal Reserve meeting caused a significant increase in Treasury yields and a decline in the S&P 500. These movements are closely tied to President-elect Trump's agenda. The market response indicates potential concerns and uncertainties regarding Trump's upcoming policies and their impact on the economy. The Federal Reserve's decisions often have a strong influence on market movements, and in this case, the relationship with Trump's agenda further amplified the effects. As a result, investors are advised to brace for possible volatility in the stock market as discussions around Trump's plans continue.

news.headline: Analyst initiates IonQ coverage with Buy rating and $50 price target, citing superior reliability of trapped-ion qubit architecture.
news.summary: An analyst from D.A. Davidson & Co., Alexander Platt, has started coverage on IonQ, Inc. (NYSE:IONQ) with a Buy rating and a price target of $50. Platt views IonQ as a prominent investment opportunity in the quantum computing sector, benefiting from the limitations of classical computing. The company's trapped-ion qubit architecture is highlighted for its reliability, accuracy, and scalability surpassing competitor designs. This comes amidst challenges faced by industry giants like IBM and Google in terms of inconsistencies and short operational lifespans. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Fubo TV launches Hallmark+ as standalone or add-on option, expanding partnership. Subscribers can access Hallmark+ without base channel plan.
news.summary: In New York, FuboTV Inc. (NYSE: FUBO) has introduced Hallmark+ as a new premium subscription option for its customers. This follows the companies' previous agreement for Hallmark's linear channels on Fubo's platform. Users now have the choice to opt for Hallmark+ as a standalone service, independent of the Fubo channel plan, or as an additional feature to their current subscription. This move highlights Fubo's commitment to enhancing its offerings for viewers, particularly in the realm of premium content.

The tray enhances the leakproof feature of Sleepypod carriers and serves as a barrier between pets and the Ultra Plush bedding. news.headline: House Republicans are eyeing a funding plan b that excludes a debt ceiling increase and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year under reconciliation — The Hill.
Sen. Lummis proposes US gov buy 200K Bitcoins/year for 5 years, to create "Strategic Bitcoin Reserve" for USD backing and national debt offsetting.
news.summary: Senator Cynthia Lummis, a Republican from Wyoming, has put forth a proposal to authorize the Federal Reserve to possess Bitcoin. The plan involves establishing a "Strategic Bitcoin Reserve" by purchasing 200,000 Bitcoins annually for five years, with the intention of holding them for a minimum of twenty years. This reserve would have a dual role: supporting the U.S. dollar, which is the global reserve currency, and reducing the national debt, currently exceeding $36 trillion. In a recent interview, Senator Lummis emphasized the importance of this reserve, emphasizing its potential to bolster the U.S. dollar and act as a long-term savings fund to mitigate economic challenges.

news.headline: Stifel upgrades rating on DT Midstream to Buy, raises price target to $106 citing growth potential, EBITDA over $1 billion.
news.summary: Financial expert Selman Akyol from Stifel has upgraded DT Midstream, Inc. (NYSE:DTM) from Hold to Buy status, while also increasing the price target significantly from $89 to $106. Akyol views the recent dip in stock price as a favorable opportunity for potential investors. DT Midstream's strategic move in acquiring a natural gas pipeline positions the company as a prominent player in the natural gas infrastructure sector. This acquisition is forecasted to boost DTM's EBITDA to over $1 billion and broaden its customer base, potentially prompting a credit rating improvement to investment grade by S&P. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Merck's two-drug regime for HIV meets goals in Phase 3 trials, includes doravirine/islatravir (DOR/ISL). Non-inferior to bART and BIC/FTC/TAF.
news.summary: Merck & Co Inc (NYSE:MRK) recently announced top-line results from two important Phase 3 trials involving a doravirine/islatravir (DOR/ISL) two-drug regimen for adults with HIV-1 infection. The trials assessed patients who were already virologically suppressed on various antiretroviral therapy regimens, including baseline antiretroviral therapy (bART) and Bictegravir/Emtricitabine/Tenofovir Alafenamide (BIC/FTC/TAF). In open-label trial MK-8591A-051, DOR/ISL proved to be just as effective as bART, while in the double-blind trial MK-8591A-052, it was shown to be non-inferior to BIC/FTC/TAF. For a more detailed report, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Willis Lease Finance Corporation purchases 9 advanced GTF engines for Airbus A320 Family, valued at $200 million each.
news.summary: In Coconut Creek, Florida, on December 19, 2024, Willis Lease Finance Corporation (NASDAQ: WLFC), a leading lessor of commercial aircraft engines and provider of global aviation service operations, announced a significant transaction with Pratt & Whitney. This transaction involves the purchase of nine new PW1133G-JM aircraft engines, with all acquisitions scheduled to be completed by December 31, 2024. Pratt & Whitney's GTF engines offer up to 20% greater fuel efficiency compared to older engines, are approved for operation using 50% sustainable aviation fuel ("SAF"), and have been effectively tested with 100% SAF. Valued at around $200 million according to Pratt & Whitney's 2024 list prices, this deal marks a substantial advancement in delivering modern and sought-after engines to airlines for the long term. It is important to note that Pratt & Whitney operates as an RTX business on the NYSE under the ticker symbol RTX.
